pleiodb rho matrix examination

Code 
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, "../src")

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.colors as mcolors
import seaborn as sns
from scipy.cluster.hierarchy import linkage, dendrogram, leaves_list
from scipy.spatial.distance import squareform
from pleiodb.db import GWASDatabase

db = GWASDatabase.open("/local-scratch/data/pleiodb/main.pleiodb")
T = db.T
traits = pd.read_csv("/local-scratch/data/pleiodb/main.pleiodb/traits.tsv", sep="\t")
print(f"T = {T}, traits table shape: {traits.shape}")
T = 4159, traits table shape: (4159, 13)
Code 
idx = list(range(T))
rho = db.get_rho_block(idx, idx)
print(f"rho matrix: {rho.shape}, dtype={rho.dtype}")
upper_i, upper_j = np.triu_indices(T, k=1)
upper = rho[upper_i, upper_j]
print(f"Upper triangle: {len(upper):,} pairs, {np.isnan(upper).sum():,} NaN")
rho matrix: (4159, 4159), dtype=float32
Upper triangle: 8,646,561 pairs, 8,316 NaN

Distribution of rho values

Code 
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 4))

vals = upper[~np.isnan(upper)]

axes[0].hist(vals, bins=200, color="steelblue", edgecolor="none")
axes[0].set_xlabel("rho")
axes[0].set_ylabel("pairs")
axes[0].set_title(f"Distribution of rho ({len(vals):,} pairs)")
axes[0].axvline(0, color="black", lw=0.8, ls="--")

axes[1].hist(vals, bins=200, color="steelblue", edgecolor="none")
axes[1].set_yscale("log")
axes[1].set_xlabel("rho")
axes[1].set_ylabel("pairs (log scale)")
axes[1].set_title("Distribution of rho (log y)")
axes[1].axvline(0, color="black", lw=0.8, ls="--")

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

print(f"Mean:   {vals.mean():.4f}")
print(f"Median: {np.median(vals):.4f}")
print(f"SD:     {vals.std():.4f}")
print(f"|rho| > 0.1: {(np.abs(vals) > 0.1).sum():,}  ({100*(np.abs(vals)>0.1).mean():.1f}%)")
print(f"|rho| > 0.3: {(np.abs(vals) > 0.3).sum():,}  ({100*(np.abs(vals)>0.3).mean():.2f}%)")
print(f"|rho| > 0.5: {(np.abs(vals) > 0.5).sum():,}  ({100*(np.abs(vals)>0.5).mean():.3f}%)")
Mean:   0.0049
Median: 0.0025
SD:     0.0474
|rho| > 0.1: 155,560  (1.8%)
|rho| > 0.3: 32,448  (0.38%)
|rho| > 0.5: 13,015  (0.151%)

Highest and lowest rho pairs

Code 
pairs = pd.DataFrame({
    "trait_id_1": traits.iloc[upper_i]["trait_id"].values,
    "trait_name_1": traits.iloc[upper_i]["trait_name"].values,
    "trait_id_2": traits.iloc[upper_j]["trait_id"].values,
    "trait_name_2": traits.iloc[upper_j]["trait_name"].values,
    "rho": upper,
}).dropna(subset=["rho"])

Top 30 positive rho pairs

Code 
top_pos = pairs.nlargest(30, "rho")[["trait_name_1", "trait_name_2", "rho"]].reset_index(drop=True)
top_pos["rho"] = top_pos["rho"].round(3)
top_pos.index += 1
top_pos

top_neg = pairs.nsmallest(30, "rho")[["trait_name_1", "trait_name_2", "rho"]].reset_index(drop=True)
top_neg["rho"] = top_neg["rho"].round(3)
top_neg.index += 1

Clustered heatmap (top 200 traits by |rho| with others)

To keep the heatmap tractable, we select the 200 traits that have the highest mean |rho| with all other traits.

Code 
mean_abs_rho = np.nanmean(np.abs(rho), axis=1)
top200_idx = np.argsort(mean_abs_rho)[::-1][:200]
top200_idx_sorted = np.sort(top200_idx)

rho_sub = rho[np.ix_(top200_idx_sorted, top200_idx_sorted)]
labels = traits.iloc[top200_idx_sorted]["trait_name"].str[:40].values

# Replace NaN with 0 for clustering distance
rho_sub_nonan = np.where(np.isnan(rho_sub), 0.0, rho_sub)
np.fill_diagonal(rho_sub_nonan, 1.0)

# Hierarchical clustering on dissimilarity 1 - |rho|
dist = squareform(1.0 - np.abs(rho_sub_nonan), checks=False)
Z = linkage(dist, method="average")
order = leaves_list(Z)

rho_ord = rho_sub_nonan[np.ix_(order, order)]
labels_ord = labels[order]

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 12))
cmap = sns.diverging_palette(220, 20, as_cmap=True)
im = ax.imshow(rho_ord, aspect="auto", cmap=cmap, vmin=-1, vmax=1,
               interpolation="nearest")
ax.set_xticks(range(200))
ax.set_yticks(range(200))
ax.set_xticklabels(labels_ord, fontsize=3, rotation=90)
ax.set_yticklabels(labels_ord, fontsize=3)
plt.colorbar(im, ax=ax, shrink=0.6, label="rho")
ax.set_title("Clustered rho heatmap — top 200 traits by mean |rho|")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

Trait-level rho summary

Code 
traits_rho = traits.copy()
traits_rho["mean_abs_rho"] = np.nanmean(np.abs(rho), axis=1)
traits_rho["max_abs_rho"]  = np.nanmax(np.abs(rho - np.eye(T)), axis=1)
traits_rho["n_high_rho"]   = (np.abs(rho - np.eye(T)) > 0.3).sum(axis=1)

print("Top 20 traits by mean |rho| with all others:")
(traits_rho[["trait_id", "trait_name", "mean_abs_rho", "max_abs_rho", "n_high_rho"]]
 .nlargest(20, "mean_abs_rho")
 .round(3)
 .reset_index(drop=True))

Code 
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 4))

axes[0].hist(traits_rho["mean_abs_rho"], bins=100, color="steelblue", edgecolor="none")
axes[0].set_xlabel("mean |rho| across all other traits")
axes[0].set_ylabel("traits")
axes[0].set_title("Per-trait mean |rho|")

axes[1].hist(traits_rho["n_high_rho"], bins=60, color="tomato", edgecolor="none")
axes[1].set_xlabel("number of traits with |rho| > 0.3")
axes[1].set_ylabel("traits")
axes[1].set_title("Per-trait high-|rho| partner count")

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
